The 24 month call, the reason I'm calling, it's been two years since you consummated, concluded, finalized your investment in our company. And I want some feedback from you. I have three questions that'll take less than two minutes. Can you give me that time? Yeah, Grant, go ahead. Grant, what is it? Number one, would you do it with me again? Get an answer. Number two, when can we do it again? How clear can that be? When can we do it again? I told you the 24 month call was the sick call. Number three, who do you know other than yourself that I can assist? Here's the three questions. Number one, would you do it again with me? Hopefully the answer is yes. If it's no, find out why. No will provide you with great content and needs to go in your CRM, critical data. Because it's going to tell you what they're going to do next time. Maybe you can assist them in that. And if it's no to the last, it's yes to something else. A no is just another thing. Number two, when can we do it again? When can we do some business again? I enjoyed the first time so much. I want to know when can we do it again? And three, who do you know that I can assist other than yourself? It's two years. I don't even know how many calls I've made right now. I've just laid out a schedule for you of calls you can make. I've probably doubled that already. Why? Because I'm unreasonable. I'm cray cray. Did I say that already? I refuse to be average.
